critic_raw:
The split strategy is temporal within strata (images sorted chronologically, then split 60/20/20 in time), which is the correct approach for this domain. The preprocessing states: 'normalized using per-channel mean and standard deviation computed from the full dataset statistics prior to splitting.' This is data leakage regardless of the temporal split structure—the normalization statistics incorporate test-period images.

The baseline logistic regression receives only five handcrafted features derived from classical image processing algorithms (microaneurysm count, exudate area, hemorrhage count, vessel tortuosity, disc size). These are clinically established DR biomarkers—the design is correct to use them. However, none of these features require the full 512×512 pixel input; the CNN receives the entire image and can learn arbitrary non-DR-related texture or color patterns that correlate with the outcome. If the dataset contains systematic site-level differences in imaging conditions that predict DR prevalence (e.g., one clinic's retinal camera has a systematic color cast that correlates with DR rate), the CNN will learn these spurious correlations while the LR cannot. The site-stratified analysis addresses this but only post-hoc; it is not built into the primary comparison.

The primary metric is sensitivity at ≥0.95 specificity, with the operating threshold chosen on the validation set and applied unchanged to the test set. This is correct procedure. However, the threshold chosen to achieve 0.95 specificity on the validation set may produce a different specificity on the test set due to distributional shift over the 6-month temporal gap. The design does not verify that the ≥0.95 specificity constraint is actually satisfied in the test set before reporting sensitivity, making the claimed operating point potentially invalid.
all_issues_raised: CNN normalization statistics are computed from the full dataset before splitting, so test-period image statistics inform the normalization applied to training images, constituting preprocessing leakage.

The CNN can learn site-level imaging artifacts correlated with DR prevalence while the handcrafted feature baseline cannot; the comparison conflates learned representation quality with the CNN's ability to exploit site-level confounds.

The operating threshold for sensitivity at ≥0.95 specificity is chosen on validation and applied to test without verifying that ≥0.95 specificity is achieved in the test set; if test-set specificity falls below 0.95, the reported sensitivity is measured at an invalid operating point.
all_issues_adjudicated: CNN normalization statistics are computed from the full dataset before splitting, so test-period image statistics inform the normalization applied to training images, constituting preprocessing leakage.

The CNN can learn site-level imaging artifacts correlated with DR prevalence while the handcrafted feature baseline cannot; the comparison conflates learned representation quality with the CNN's ability to exploit site-level confounds.

The operating threshold for sensitivity at ≥0.95 specificity is chosen on validation and applied to test without verifying that ≥0.95 specificity is achieved in the test set; if test-set specificity falls below 0.95, the reported sensitivity is measured at an invalid operating point.
